tactical_shape: Attacked through full-backs and wide service, with João Cancelo reaching the byline and Nuno Mendes delivering from the left.
Used set-piece routines creatively, including a rehearsed free-kick trick and low-swinging corner deliveries.
Bruno Fernandes operated with freedom in midfield, exploiting a vacant central area on one goal.
standout_players: Cristiano Ronaldo: back on the scoresheet with a first-half brace; the focal point of the attack and the loudest headline act.
João Cancelo: provided the byline cross for Ronaldo's opener.
Nuno Mendes: supplied the disguised free-kick that became Ronaldo's second goal.
Bruno Fernandes: influential creator, playing in Ronaldo for the third and delivering the corner that led to the fourth.
Rafael Leão: scored Portugal's final goal with a rising drive.
Austin McPhee (set-piece coach): credited alongside Martínez for successful set-piece work.
home_read.momentum: Portugal looked rampant and comfortable, with creative set pieces and Ronaldo rediscovering his scoring touch, though the match raises few answers about tougher tests ahead.

tactical_shape: Set up to defend against a far superior opponent but were described as utterly unequipped to repel Portugal's quality.
Briefly rallied after going behind, but offered little sustained resistance.
standout_players: Abdukodir Khusanov: made a dramatic intervention to prevent Ronaldo's hat-trick and was later unlucky in the own-goal sequence.
Abduvokhid Nematov: beaten by the set-piece trick and then involved in the corner goal concession; could not keep Portugal out.
fatigue_injuries: Odiljon Khamrobekov conceded the free-kick after a desperate foul on Pedro Neto.
away_read.momentum: A heavy defeat and a performance judged well below the level required, with little sign they could cope with elite opposition.
